atlas.put_in: the stabilized flow at a finer grid with fixed vs calibrated kappa (~dx^4); the law is not put in
emerged: fixed kappa: CV=3.5% ; calibrated kappa~dx^4: CV=3.7%
NEGATIVE RESULT: the kappa~dx^4 calibration did NOT improve the fit (calibrated CV >= fixed CV) -- the high-L degradation is NOT explained by simple kappa scaling (H001 dead-end)
surprises: the kappa~dx^4 hypothesis was TESTED and REFUTED: calibration did not recover a tighter law -- an honest dead-end, recorded
atlas.persistence: size law is tight (CV~3.5%) at L=44/52; the kappa calibration does not change that
atlas.not_scripted_check: sizes from the flow; CV from size/sqrt(c4); only a kappa scaling rule is put in
atlas.claim_tier: measured (calibration did NOT help = negative result) ; interpretive (kappa scaling insufficient) ; analogy (particle)
floors: the size LAW is tight at L=44/52 (CV~3.5%); the kappa~dx^4 fix did NOT recover it at higher L (tested, negative)
true any-start->L* globalization remains open (H001); absolute k stays resolution-dependent; fixed lattice
